steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb of a ‘that’ clause: ‘It is widely known that New York City’s Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) ______ a vast collection.’ The subject of that clause is ‘Museum of Modern Art’ (singular). The blank needs a finite verb in the correct form to serve as the clause’s predicate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "housing": A present participle. Participles are non-finite and cannot stand alone as a clause’s main verb; inserting it leaves the ‘that’ clause without a predicate. ✗
  • "to house": An infinitive. Infinitives are also non-finite and cannot function as a clause’s main verb. ✗
  • "to have housed": A perfect infinitive. Still non-finite — cannot serve as the clause’s predicate. ✗
  • "houses": A finite verb in the third-person singular present tense, matching the singular subject ‘Museum of Modern Art.’ It correctly fills the predicate slot in the ‘that’ clause. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
